title: 12 Reinstating Cheques on ORS
body_text:
12	Reinstating Cheques on ORS
12.1	Overview of Reinstating Cheques on ORS
ORS users can update incorrect cheque information on ORS (i.e., the Transit Number, account number, or cheque number). This is known as reinstating a cheque.
The benefit of reinstating cheques is that it allows users to search the ORS database using the correct information and improves the match rate for cheque image statements (Image Files). Reinstating cheques isnât mandatory.
Some uses for the ORS reinstate function include:
Updating the transit/account number for a cheque that is redirected from one branch/account to another branch/account on the banking system.
Adding an account number/cheque number for a cheque that isnât MICR encoded (a PAD or counter cheque).
Correcting an account number for a cheque where it was misread due to poor MICR encoding.
12.2	Creating Reinstated Cheques on ORS
Proceed as follows to reinstate a cheque on ORS:
Log in to ORS and locate the cheque that you want to reinstate. See Section 7.2, Searching for Cheques on ORS.
Select the
R
link in the A column. See Figure 1.
Note:	The
R
link is also available on the Search View page.
Figure 1: Select Cheque
Complete the Edit Cheque Reinstate page as follows. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Edit Cheque Reinstate
â
Reinstate Transit
â To update the Transit Number associated with the image, select a new Transit Number from the drop-down list.
â
Reinstate Account
â To update the account number associated with the image, enter a new account number (maximum 14 digits).
â
Reinstate Cheque Number
â To update the cheque number, enter a new cheque number (maximum 12 digits).
Note:	Fields that are left blank will retain the original information.
Select
Submit
. The Search View or Search Results page opens depending upon the page from which the R link was selected. The reinstate status is displayed in the RS column. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Reinstate Status
P
= Pending
C
= Complete
12.3	Editing Reinstated Cheques on ORS
Proceed as follows to edit a reinstated cheque on ORS:
Log in to ORS and select the
Summary
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Summary Option
Select the
R
link in the A column beside the reinstated cheque you want to edit. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Return Summary â Current
Note:	The
R
link is also available from the Search Results
or Search View
page.
Make the necessary changes (e.g., enter a different cheque number).
Select
Submit
.
Note:	The reinstated Transit Number, account number, and/or cheque number wonât show in the grid until Central 1 runs the overnight process on ORS. The updated information will appear on ORS the following business day.
12.4	Cancelling Reinstated Cheques on ORS
Proceed as follows to cancel a reinstated cheque on ORS:
Log in to ORS and select the
Summary
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Summary Option
Select the
R
link in the A column beside the reinstated cheque you want to delete. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Return Summary â Current
Note:	The R link is also available from the Search Results
or Search View
pages.
Select
Delete
.
Note:	If the deleted cheque return also has a RI status of P or F, the item wonât disappear from the Return Summary â Current or Return Summary â Forward
page. However, the RS status changes from P to blank.
END
